what_it_does: Implements the Project View workspace as three pages: ProjectOverview (dashboard with lifecycle, stats, activity, and a Vi note), ProjectContent (gallery of mockups grouped by approval status), and ProjectNotes (feed of decisions, threads, and open questions). It also defines two small helper components, PVStat and PVActivity, for the overview cards and activity rows.
observations: Registers components as window globals: ProjectOverview, ProjectContent, ProjectNotes, PVStat, PVActivity.
Uses window.ViContext.set to report workspace context on mount.
Lifecycle stages are hardcoded with percentages, icons, notes, and navigation targets.
Overview stats, activity feed, and notes are static arrays rather than derived from props or MS_DATA.
Content page filters data.mockups by status into approved, style_anchor, and candidate groups.
Some buttons are non-functional placeholders (Filter, Add a note).
Heavy use of inline style objects alongside CSS utility classes.
Vibe page is referenced but not implemented in this file.
standouts: The lifecycle stage rail combines icon, label, progress bar, note, and arrow into a single clickable navigator.
A synthesized 'Vi note' gives a strategic recommendation about closing the last two states.
Content is grouped by material status (canonical, style anchors, candidates) with badge thumbnails.
conventions: Context reporting via useEffect on mount.
Presentational helper components defined below their consumers.
Mix of CSS class utilities and inline style objects.
concerns: Large amount of hardcoded data makes the file read like a prototype or mock.
Lifecycle percentages and stats do not appear derived from project data.
Notes list uses array index as React key.
Several interactive buttons have no attached handlers.
No propTypes or TypeScript types for component props.
notes: A project-level dashboard workspace that mixes lifecycle navigation, content status galleries, and a decision notes feed, wired to global navigation but still carrying a lot of static sample data.
questions: Where is the Vibe page implemented, since it is referenced but absent here?
Are the lifecycle stage percentages and notes intended to be computed from project data?
What is the full shape of window.MS_DATA and how does it relate to the static stats?
How are the navigation zones (sequence, build, asset, dashboard) mapped in the parent router?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: the file provides a visual, whole-project view covering lifecycle position, content status, and notes, though it delegates the Vibe page to another file.
apparent_themes: Running memory of decisions and open questions
Synthesized assistant guidance embedded in the UI
intention_signals: To create a command-center view of a design project that lets a user grasp status, browse generated material, and read the evolving rationale behind decisions, while nudging toward next actions.
novelty: The lifecycle rail treats project phases as a continuous, clickable progression with per-stage progress bars and contextual notes, and the inline 'Vi note' acts as an embedded strategic advisor.
contribution: Provides the project overview shell, lifecycle navigator, content status grouping, and notes feed patterns that a fused system would retain as the project-level dashboard surface.
summary_for_embedding: React workspace file defining ProjectOverview, ProjectContent, and ProjectNotes components for a project-level dashboard with lifecycle stages, content galleries, activity feed, and decision notes.
intention_for_embedding: To present the whole project as a navigable, visual command center showing lifecycle position, generated material status, and running decisions.

symbols.does: Renders a numeric stat with an optional gold tone and a muted label inside a quiet card.
symbols.description: PVStat is a small presentational component used in the overview grid. It displays a large value and a label, applying a gold accent color when the tone prop is 'gold' and default ink otherwise.

symbols.does: Renders a single activity row with an icon, tone class, description, and relative timestamp.
symbols.description: PVActivity is a presentational list-item component for the recent activity panel. It combines an icon span styled by a tone class, descriptive text, and an 'ago' timestamp using the qa-row layout classes.
